resources.string:
• Tap a device action or switch group to run it
• Optional device and group colors fill the tile; they do not indicate action or Wi-Fi status
• Choose list or widget view when there is enough space for readable widgets
• Arrows change the dashboard order
• The X at the bottom right cancels a running action
• Empty switch groups are shown but cannot be run
• Action details: tap the compact bar to open it, or its title to minimize it
• Green dot: Wi-Fi connection confirmed
• Red dot: a specific Wi-Fi connection attempt failed
• Gray dot: Wi-Fi not yet confirmed, no assignment, or detection unavailable
• A visible SSID does not confirm a connection
• The menu opens settings, updates, help, and About SwitchWerk

• Wi-Fi profiles: manage credentials
• Devices: define actions and Wi-Fi assignments
• Groups: run multiple existing device actions in sequence
• System: appearance, updates, action details, and external intents
• Configurations: export or import the configuration

• Choose appearance and language
• Check for updates and open GitHub release notes
• Show or hide action details
• Allow or block external intents

• Export saves Wi-Fi profiles, devices, switch groups, and transferable app settings
• Device and switch group shortcut selections are transferred
• Homescreen widget assignments stay local to this Android launcher
• Switch group members, order, and pauses are transferred
• Language, display, Wi-Fi profile sorting, and external intent permission are transferred
• Setup wizard state remains local
• Changes to external intent permission are shown before import confirmation
• Choose password handling deliberately for each action
• The password choice applies only to the next action and is reset afterwards
Wi-Fi passwords are stored encrypted on this device. They are not sent to a cloud service and are not shown in action details.
Enter the device\'s local address in its assigned Wi-Fi network, for example 192.168.33.1 or a hostname resolved on the local network. No internet connection is required.
The HTTP/RPC call is appended to the local device address. Use only a path supported by the device. POST can send an optional request body with a Content-Type, for example /relay/0?turn=on.

• Shows actions from the dashboard, widgets, app shortcuts, and external intents
• Shows Wi-Fi connection, timeout, and HTTP status
• Layout values remain available as the oldest scrollable log entry
• The X at the bottom right cancels the running dashboard action
• Tap the compact bar to open it
• Tap the Action details title in the open panel to minimize it
• Local progress only
• Passwords and device addresses are not shown

• Groups run existing device actions in sequence
• Groups can optionally use the same fixed color palette as devices
• The same device can be added more than once
• Empty groups are visible on the dashboard but cannot be run
• Use arrows to set member order
• The pause applies after that member action

• Use + to add an entry
• Tap an entry to edit it
• Swipe left or right to delete it
• Confirm deletion afterwards
• Enter the target Wi-Fi SSID\n• The name must be unique\n• The password remains encrypted on the device

• Set the name and button label
• Optionally choose a device color; standard keeps the existing appearance
• Optionally offer the action as an app shortcut
• Android shows at most four enabled shortcuts in dashboard order
• Choose HTTP or HTTPS
• Enter the HTTP/RPC method and path
• For POST, optionally choose request body and Content-Type
• Wi-Fi assignments define the connection order

• Set the name and button label
• Optionally choose a group color; standard keeps the existing appearance
• Optionally offer the group as an app shortcut
• Empty groups are not published as runnable shortcuts
• Dashboard order is changed with arrows on the dashboard
• Use the error switch to stop on the first error or continue after errors
• Add existing devices as members; the same device can appear more than once
• Members run from top to bottom
• Pause is applied after the member action
• Custom pauses use HH:MM:SS.mmm and are limited to 1 hour
Offer this device action when the app icon is long-pressed. Android shows at most four selected actions.
Offer this switch group when the app icon is long-pressed. Android shows at most four selected shortcuts in dashboard order.

• Enable a device action or switch group in its dialog
• Long-press the app icon and select the shortcut
• At most four enabled non-empty shortcuts are offered in dashboard order
• Progress and results appear on the dashboard

• Add a SwitchWerk widget from the Android launcher
• Select one or more local device actions or non-empty switch groups; their selection numbers define the widget order
• Long-press the widget and use the launcher edit action, if available, to change its settings
• If the launcher offers no edit action, remove and add the widget again
• Choose automatic, one-column, or two-column button layout when editing
• One column stacks broad buttons when the widget is tall enough
• New widgets use the title SwitchWerk; leave it empty to give its space to the action buttons, or use the reset icon to restore it
• A tap immediately shows the running state; additional taps on the same running action do not start it again
• Light green or red shows the action result for four seconds, then the current Wi-Fi status color returns
• Taps run through the same local action logic without visibly opening SwitchWerk
• Widget assignments are launcher-local and are not exported or imported

• Enable external intents in Settings → System
• Find stable local device and switch group IDs in an exported configuration
• Only existing local device or switch group IDs are accepted
• Empty switch groups are rejected
• URLs, commands, request bodies, and additional extras are rejected
• The intent opens SwitchWerk in the foreground
• Progress and results appear on the dashboard
• Error notices disappear automatically but remain in Action details until cleared

External device action (replace device-123 with the local device ID):
adb shell am start -n de.piecha.switchwerk/.MainActivity -a de.piecha.switchwerk.action.RUN_DEVICE_ACTION --es de.piecha.switchwerk.extra.DEVICE_ID device-123

External switch group action (replace group-123 with the local switch group ID):
adb shell am start -n de.piecha.switchwerk/.MainActivity -a de.piecha.switchwerk.action.RUN_GROUP_ACTION --es de.piecha.switchwerk.extra.GROUP_ID group-123

External device action without ID (error test):
adb shell am start -n de.piecha.switchwerk/.MainActivity -a de.piecha.switchwerk.action.RUN_DEVICE_ACTION

External switch group action without ID (error test):
adb shell am start -n de.piecha.switchwerk/.MainActivity -a de.piecha.switchwerk.action.RUN_GROUP_ACTION
Disabled by default. Other apps can start configured device actions and non-empty switch groups by local ID only. Error notices disappear automatically and remain in Action details until cleared.
External intents are disabled in Settings.
The external intent has no device ID.
The external intent has no switch group ID.
The external intent references an unknown device.
The external intent references an unknown switch group.
The external intent references an empty switch group.
The external intent is invalid and was rejected.
• Select a Wi-Fi profile for the device\n• Enter the local device address\n• No internet access is required

SwitchWerk switches Wi-Fi devices that are not part of another network and cannot be reached through it. If these devices provide their own small Wi-Fi network, SwitchWerk can connect to it and trigger actions. You need to know which actions the devices support; this information is usually available from the manufacturer or relevant internet forums.\n\nYour configuration stays on this device. SwitchWerk works locally and does not require accounts, cloud services or tracking.

• Existing configuration: open Settings → Configurations and import a configuration
• Manual setup 1: create the Wi-Fi profiles SwitchWerk should connect to in Settings → Wi-Fi Profiles
• Manual setup 2: create devices in Settings → Devices and assign them to a Wi-Fi profile
• Optional: create switch groups in Settings → Groups
• Trigger the configured actions on the Dashboard

• The status point confirms a Wi-Fi connection, not physical Wi-Fi proximity
• Green: Wi-Fi connection confirmed
• Red: Android could not establish the specific Wi-Fi request
• Gray: Wi-Fi not yet confirmed, no assignment, or detection unavailable
• A visible SSID alone does not confirm a connection
• The X at the bottom right cancels a running action and its Wi-Fi request
• Android's system dialog cannot be closed by SwitchWerk
• A device access point may interrupt internet access
• HTTP errors come from the device

• Import configurations from trusted sources only
• Wi-Fi profiles, devices, switch groups, shortcut selections, language, display, Wi-Fi profile sorting, and external intent permission can be transferred
• Homescreen widget assignments remain local to the Android launcher and are not transferred
• Switch groups may reference only existing or imported devices; a device may appear more than once
• Setup wizard state remains local
• Changes to external intent permission are shown before import confirmation
• Password import and export must be selected deliberately before each action: left without passwords, right with passwords
• The related action stays disabled in the middle position
• The password choice applies only to the next action and is reset afterwards

• Release updates are checked through the official GitHub project
• GitHub release notes open the release page in the browser
• Automatic checks run at most once per day
• APK download and installation start only after user action
• Local operation only\n• No accounts, cloud, or tracking\n• Wi-Fi and location permissions only for Wi-Fi features
When you open a linked page from this wizard, Back returns here until you close the wizard with one of the buttons at the bottom.
SwitchWerk switches Wi-Fi devices that are not part of another network and cannot be reached through it.

1. In Settings → Wi-Fi Profiles, configure the Wi-Fi networks SwitchWerk should connect to.\n2. In Settings → Devices, create devices and assign them to a Wi-Fi profile.\n3. On the app home screen (Dashboard), trigger the configured actions.

The colored LED-style indicators show the last known connection status: green means confirmed, red means the connection failed, and gray means unknown or not assigned. Treat them as guidance only, because Android policies can limit Wi-Fi detection and connection feedback.
Do you want to import an existing configuration?
Open Configurations if you want to load a prepared file, URL or QR code.
Do you want to set up devices yourself?
Use the three steps in order if you want to create Wi-Fi profiles and devices manually.
View app information, version, and a link to the GitHub project.
Layout: screen %1$d dp, dashboard %2$d dp, font %3$.2f, widget min. %4$d dp, selector %5$s
Debug builds are not treated as updateable release versions.
Debug builds do not check for release updates.
The matching APK asset is missing.
The release contains multiple matching APK assets.
Update check failed because of a network error.
GitHub could not provide the release data.
The Android installation dialog could not be opened.
The GitHub release page could not be opened.
No device action has been run yet
No Wi-Fi assignment yet. Use + to add a Wi-Fi profile and hostname/IP.
Use the arrows to reorder. Switching uses the order from top to bottom.
No additional Wi-Fi profiles are available.
Off: stop group on first error
No members yet. Use + to add existing devices.
Use the arrows to reorder. The group runs from top to bottom.

plurals.name: delete_wifi_profile_used_warning
plurals.item: %1$d device will lose its Wi-Fi assignment.
%1$d devices will lose their Wi-Fi assignment.

resources.string: An empty password is allowed. The existing password is only retained if the asterisks remain unchanged.
This Wi-Fi network must be configured and connected in Android. SwitchWerk does not store credentials.
Please connect to the Wi-Fi network “%1$s” in Android. Then return to SwitchWerk and run the device action again.
Select SSID from visible Wi-Fi networks
Save configurations as a JSON file or import them from a trusted source.
Select a source and load the configuration.
The export file contains Wi-Fi passwords in plain text. Only share it with people who are allowed to know these passwords.
Load an existing configuration from a file, URL or QR code.
Existing entries are retained. Matching IDs are overwritten.
All local devices, Wi-Fi profiles, and passwords are deleted first.
Will be enabled by this import.
Will be disabled by this import.
%1$d new, %2$d existing entries will be replaced
Choose whether passwords should be imported.
The loaded configuration contains no password values. Choose without passwords to continue.
Next step: start Import or use Cancel on the right.
Password values will be applied as shown in the summary.
Password fields are ignored; existing passwords are retained when adding entries.
%1$d with a value will be imported, %2$d empty fields will delete existing values
%1$d local Wi-Fi profiles will be deleted.
Profile name must not be empty
Profile name is already in use
Wi-Fi profile could not be saved
Wi-Fi profile could not be deleted
QR code does not contain a valid HTTP/HTTPS URL
Camera permission is required to scan the QR code
This Wi-Fi profile is already assigned
Device name must not be empty
Button label must not be empty
API call must not be empty
Device order could not be changed
Switch group name must not be empty
Switch group could not be saved
Switch group could not be deleted
SwitchWerk device and switch group actions
Select local device actions and non-empty switch groups. Selection numbers define their widget order. Leave the title empty to use the space for actions; the reset icon restores SwitchWerk.
No executable device actions or switch groups are configured.
Action was removed or is empty
The selected target is no longer available
Wi-Fi security type could not be detected; using fallback
The device hostname could not be resolved
The device IP address is unreachable
Wi-Fi request timed out; no connection established
Group stopped: device for step %1$d is missing
No Wi-Fi profile configured for device
Android-managed Wi-Fi is not active. Please connect to the target Wi-Fi network in Android.
Wi-Fi password is missing. Save it again in the Wi-Fi profile.
Android does not support this Wi-Fi connection method
The device HTTPS certificate could not be verified
Device is unreachable on the network
Access blocked by VPN or firewall
Failed to access device over the network
Wi-Fi detection unavailable: Wi-Fi is disabled
Wi-Fi detection unavailable: system location services are disabled
Wi-Fi detection unavailable: permission is missing
